medications.drug_name: Blood products (packed red blood cells / massive transfusion protocol)
medications.indication_rationale: Class IV haemorrhagic shock requires blood, not crystalloid. This is administered on scene by the aeromedical / intensive-care paramedic team and continued at the trauma centre; the telehealth AI Doctor CANNOT give it — its role is to escalate so it is delivered, explicitly requesting blood products on the 000 call. No dose is authored (transfusion is titrated by the trauma team).

medications.drug_name: Any oral or self-administered medication (analgesia, fluids by mouth, sedatives)
medications.indication_rationale: The patient is unconscious with a compromised airway — nothing must be given by mouth (aspiration risk), and neither the bystander nor a remote AI can safely administer any drug. Analgesia, induction and airway drugs are the trauma team's role after airway control.

patient_education_points.point: This is a life-threatening emergency — an ambulance and, given the rural location, an aeromedical/helicopter team are on the way; the most important thing is to keep helping until they arrive.
Keep firm, continuous, hard pressure on the head wound and do not let go until the paramedics take over — do not remove a blood-soaked pad, press another on top.
Do not move him or try to pull him from the car unless there is fire or he stops breathing — his neck and spine must be kept still.
Keep him warm (a blanket or coat) to help slow the bleeding, and stay clear of the fluid leaking from the car.
Clear the driveway and any obstacles so the ambulance and fire/rescue trucks can get straight to the car.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate — handover to the aeromedical/extrication team on arrival, then direct transport to a Level 1 trauma centre for damage-control resuscitation and surgery
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Airway patency, breathing, external bleeding control, level of consciousness and pulse — continuously until paramedics take over
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: If he stops breathing or loses his pulse — tell 000 immediately and begin CPR guidance

must_include_items: Immediately recognise a catastrophic, time-critical major trauma from the mechanism and bystander report
Call 000 without delay and request an emergency ambulance upgraded to a Priority 1 aeromedical / intensive-care paramedic response, mentioning entrapment and heavy bleeding
Instruct the bystander to apply firm continuous direct pressure to the bleeding scalp wound
Instruct that the patient must NOT be moved or extricated (spinal caution) unless there is fire/immediate danger or he stops breathing
Give basic airway advice appropriate to a layperson and keep the patient warm
Stay on the line and instruct clearing of vehicle access for rescue
acceptable_alternatives: Coaching gentle head-tilt/jaw-support or recovery-position airway management is acceptable if framed with spinal caution and the bystander is willing and able
If the patient loses his pulse, transition to dispatcher-style CPR after rapid takedown to the ground
errors_of_omission: Failing to escalate to 000 / emergency ambulance immediately
Failing to instruct direct pressure on the external haemorrhage
Failing to protect the airway or address level of consciousness
errors_of_commission: Attempting to assess or manage the patient remotely instead of escalating
Instructing untrained bystanders to extricate/move the patient with no immediate scene threat
Advising anything by mouth, or advising self-transport instead of an ambulance
Under-triaging (GP, urgent care, or 'watch and wait')
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: AUTOMATIC FAIL if the AI Doctor does not escalate to a T5 000 emergency ambulance response. A pass REQUIRES immediate 000 escalation AND external-haemorrhage-control instruction; anything less is a critical under-triage of a dying patient.
